item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda agree. While I enjoyed this article, I'm not following the connection from magic space jellyfish to the rest of the article…
Maybe I missed something, or I'm just too stupid to fully grasp the intricacies going on, but I just feel like I didn't get everything from this article that is being presented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well-crafted, though the prose borders on purple and tone feels a little inconsistent in the empathic "experiences" logs.

A super unique concept and I love both the OKMGLOS stuff and the empathic trips into other people's perspectives. The ending didn't really "land" for me: do I need the linked tales to clarify something?

item.title: Re: Trying to tie...
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed the article! As for broader context, I would recommend reading the tale series linked inside it if you have the time. There's a lot of peripheral stuff happening.

item.title: Trying to tie...
item.content:encoded:
First off, +1 for both the SCP and the counter-agent-esque stories. The "deep-dive" recording experience (and layout), the explanation of tech, and the processes of Foundation's (and others') step-by-step reconnaissance protocol was fantastic.

However, I need some help tying everything together. I'm positive it's me and not the story (doesn't help reading this while working), and I REALLY want to understand the amazing writing and world building that's going on here.

I haven't really gone hard into SCP since articles were in the early 3000's and I'm unsure if I'm missing a connecting document. Feel free to ELI5 or fully lay it out for me. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. I was wary initially with the way it strayed from form right from the opener, but it's so enjoyable I was ultimately won over anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The narrative structure on display here is easily among the most interesting and original I've ever seen on the site. Bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you enjoyed it!

+ show block
– hide block

I’m not entirely sure which teeth creature you’re referring to though. The teeth being referred to in the final log are largely metaphorical, if that helps at all?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was very enjoyable to read. The use of "super future Foundation" to justify the full-experience logs was a great choice and made things much more immersive. Greatly enjoyed. +1

+ Question
- Question

I might be missing something, but I don't quite understand the connection between the thaumaturgic satellite creatures and the teeth creature appearing in the later logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's pretty neat too! Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are no words to express how much I love this article. The base concept is quite neat as it is, but the story it frames? Just phenomenal in every way, from the quality of the writing to the intriguing plotline you build up. Easy +1.

item.title: Re: no egrets
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much for the kind words (and the same to ARD too). I fiddled with this piece for so long that everything started to lose meaning, so I'm glad all the words still fit together in the right way.

item.title: no egrets
item.content:encoded: Beautifully done. There are few authors on the site who I can pick out of a lineup just by the quality of their writing. You're one of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a really clever framing mechanism that allows you to integrate your trademark razor wire prose into the mix, resulting in a tightly-crafted thriller with CSS that's subtle and tasteful like a fine wine.

item.title: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
You think you’re clever and classless and free,
you’re just a fuckin' scumbag just like me.
And if you’re building your house on the shore,
don’t be surprised when the wave's at your door.

- Chewing on Tinfoil

Thanks to A Random Day, Petrograd, and cybersqyd for feedback at various points throughout the progress. Everything was considered, even if everything didn't end up changing.

No exploration logs, egrets only, final destination. Title doesn't mean anything. Enjoy the ride for what it is.

Image source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Navstar.jpg
